metadata.scenario: InviteJoy digital invitation platform — 4-email lifecycle sequence

notes: All 4 without-skill outputs failed the 'multiple_variations' assertion — consistent with iteration-1 findings. Without the skill, Claude produces a single competent email version.
All 4 with-skill outputs passed all 7 assertions, providing 3 variations each with named frameworks (BAB, PAS, PPPP, AIDA, ACCA), A/B testing recommendations, and strategic notes.

The with-skill outputs additionally provided: multiple subject line options per variation, cultural/global audience notes, timing recommendations, and personalization suggestions — none of which appeared in without-skill outputs.

Qualitative observation: with-skill outputs had stronger benefit framing (e.g., 'RSVP tracking — no spreadsheets, no guessing' vs 'RSVP tracking — Know exactly who's coming') and more deliberate persuasion psychology (loss aversion, reciprocity, specificity).
The 'multiple_variations' assertion continues to be the primary differentiator, consistently failing for without-skill and passing for with-skill across both iterations.
Token usage ~29% higher for with-skill runs due to reading skill+reference files and producing 3x more content. Time ~2.3x longer — expected tradeoff.
